text: Meeting deadlines is a key skill in both work and study. Good time management is essential, so start by breaking large tasks into smaller steps and setting mini-deadlines. Prioritization helps you focus on what is most important, especially when you have multiple projects. Sometimes, a task may be too large to complete on time; this is where scope management comes in. You can adjust the scope by reducing less critical parts to finish the core work. Finally, communication is crucial. If you foresee a problem, tell your manager or teacher early. They may offer help or extend the deadline, which is better than missing it without warning.
